medications: Naproxen 220 mg as needed (short courses for shoulder pain)
health.mental_health: Generally resilient but under persistent stress from juggling early client sessions with night classes; occasional insomnia before exams and worries about keeping up academically at 51.
recent_visits: Primary care visit (lipid panel follow-up and statin refill)
Physical therapy evaluation for shoulder pain (home exercise program)
Pharmacy blood pressure screening at a CVS (borderline readings)

finance.income_range: $55,000–$75,000/year (mix of gym payroll and private clients; fluctuates seasonally)
finance.debt: Carries about $9,800 on a credit card from a slow client season and tuition-related expenses; also on a payment plan for community college tuition and fees (~$1,200/semester).
investments: Rollover IRA from a prior job (small balance)
recent_transactions: Miami Dade College tuition payment ($642) and textbook purchase ($186)
Copay for primary care visit ($35) and atorvastatin refill ($12)
Used equipment purchase: adjustable dumbbells from OfferUp ($240)
Late fee on credit card (missed due date during midterms, $29)

relationships: Partner: Elena Rivera (44), works in hospitality; supportive but frustrated by his night-class schedule
Adult daughter: Alyssa Kowalski (23) in Tampa; they talk weekly
Study group with two younger classmates from Miami Dade College (keeps it mostly academic)
Longtime client/friend: Jorge Alvarez (mid-50s) who refers new clients
social.social_media_activity: Posts workout clips and client testimonials on Instagram and TikTok a few times a week; keeps school-related updates minimal to avoid looking distracted from training. Uses Facebook groups for local fitness networking and to find used equipment.
private_conversations: Discussing with Elena whether he should cut back client hours to protect GPA
Talking with Alyssa about feeling self-conscious being the oldest in class
Asking a trainer friend about under-the-table cash sessions and how to handle taxes
Confiding in Jorge about credit card stress and fear of losing clients while studying

schedule.work_hours: Typically 5:30am–1:30pm Mon–Fri at a Brickell-area gym; two private sessions on Saturdays (8:00am–11:00am). Keeps Sundays mostly off unless a client is traveling.
regular_appointments: Night classes Tue/Thu 6:00pm–9:15pm (Miami Dade College, Downtown campus)
Weekly physical therapy exercises at home (20–30 minutes, 5x/week) and a PT check-in every other Friday
Monthly cholesterol/BP check (pharmacy kiosk or clinic)
Standing Sunday evening call with daughter (7:30pm)
upcoming_events: Meeting with academic advisor about transferring credits (in two weeks)
Annual physical scheduled for next month
Client transformation photo shoot for gym marketing (Saturday morning, two weeks from now)
schedule.daily_routine: Up at 4:45am, quick coffee and light breakfast, trains clients through early afternoon, short nap or meal prep, homework at a café, then evening class on Tue/Thu; tries to be in bed by 10:30pm but often studies later before exams.

professional.company: Elevate Fitness Brickell (mid-size private gym with membership and personal training packages)
projects: Building a 12-week cholesterol-friendly fitness and nutrition coaching add-on for middle-aged clients
Preparing for a NASM Corrective Exercise Specialist exam to broaden services
Developing a small client portal (Google Sheets + Trainerize) to track workouts while he’s in class
Creating Spanish-language onboarding materials to grow his Miami client base
professional.performance_issues: Has had a few last-minute schedule changes due to exams and group projects; one client complained to the training manager about reduced availability. He’s also avoiding heavy demonstration lifts because of shoulder pain, which affects session flow.
workplace_relationships: Training manager (Samantha Lee) is supportive but monitoring cancellations closely
Friendly rivalry with another trainer (Dylan Price) who competes for high-paying clients
Relies on the front-desk lead (Marisol Vega) to help reshuffle appointments when class deadlines hit

hobbies: Beach runs and outdoor calisthenics at South Pointe Park
Watching UFC and boxing on weekends
Weekend cycling on the Rickenbacker Causeway
Occasional salsa nights with his partner when schedules allow
habits: Tracks macros and steps; logs blood pressure readings intermittently
Drinks 2–3 coffees most days to get through early sessions and night classes
Uses nicotine gum occasionally during high-stress weeks (trying not to go back to smoking)
Meal preps on Sundays to stay within budget and keep cholesterol in check
Studies at a café to stay focused because home feels distracting
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a one-bedroom apartment in Little Havana with his partner; keeps a small corner set up as a study desk and a few pieces of training equipment. Budget is tight due to tuition and inconsistent client volume.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly Mediterranean-leaning: lots of fish, chicken, beans, vegetables, and olive oil; limits red meat and fried foods but still has occasional late-night takeout after class. Watches saturated fat due to cholesterol.
